description: Shielding reduction factors ABOVE the baseline already included in Table 3.3 TID values
note: Table 3.3 TID numbers already assume ~10mm Al / light spacecraft shielding. These factors represent ADDITIONAL reduction from upgrading beyond that baseline. Low is the baseline (1.0x = no change). Medium and High represent heavier shielding upgrades.
confidence_note: GCR and SPE factors are supported by HZETRN data and Artemis I measurements. Trapped electron factors are low-confidence estimates: Medium is interpolated, High is anchored to Europa Clipper vault data (150 krad behind vault vs ~700 krad/yr unshielded orbit-averaged = ~0.06x at 100 mil Al, but Clipper vault is heavier than 100 mil). These should be treated as rough approximations.

levels.name: Low
levels.label: LOW
levels.description: Baseline spacecraft bus (~5-10 g/cm² Al). This is what Table 3.3 already assumes.

levels.name: Medium
levels.label: MED
levels.description: Dedicated compartment (~20 g/cm² Al). Crewed module wall.

levels.name: High
levels.label: HIGH
levels.description: Heavy vault (~40-50 g/cm² Al-eq). Europa Clipper class.
